market_summary: REGIME: Mixed/Defensive — SPY mom=+2.31%, 1m=-3.70%, below BOTH MA50 and MA200 (close=$655.38 vs MA200=$657.20) — SPY MA200 breach persists for 2nd consecutive session, confirming the regime deterioration flagged 2026-03-23. QQQ mom=+3.60%, 1m=-2.11%, below both MAs. IWM above MA200 but below MA50 (only small-cap with MA200 support). TLT below both MAs (mom=-1.25%), no bond flight-to-safety bid — stagflation/rates-pressure backdrop unchanged. BTC-USD mom=-21.02%, 3m=-23.38%, below MA200 — crypto fully risk-off. GLD above MA200 ($375.08) but sharply below MA50 ($455.73): 1m=-16.05%, dd63=-18.52% — acute precious metals correction deepening. Regime: RISK-OFF/DEFENSIVE tilt confirmed. Portfolio weighted momentum composite ~29% vs SPY +2.31% = +27pp alpha spread maintained despite gold/silver drawdowns.

LEADERSHIP: KLAC (#1, mom=+45.04%, above both MAs, 3m=+21.49%, 1m=+1.60%) remains the cleanest quality/momentum equity in the portfolio — FY2026 EPS ramp $9.14→$12.10 intact. XOM (#2, mom=+34.06%, above both MAs, 3m=+39.00%, 1m=+6.88%, dd63=-5.48%) — strongest risk-adjusted energy momentum, no flags. TSM (#3, mom=+31.97%, above MA200, 3m=+17.46%, 1m=-8.28%) — persistent MA50 breach (close=$338.45 vs MA50=$347.18) with tariff/geopolitical drag, but FY2026→FY2028 EPS $3.26→$5.24 structural thesis intact. CVX (mom=+28.92%, above both MAs, lowest vol=19.39%, dd63=-5.28%) — defensive ballast confirmed. GOOGL (mom=+25.02%, above MA200 only, 3m=-1.59%) — 3m momentum below zero is a soft flag; FY2026 EPS $2.76→$3.21 intact, antitrust headline risk persistent. LMT (mom=+24.59%, above MA200 only, 3m=+30.66%, 1m=-6.22%) — defense rearmament thesis intact, FY2026 EPS $6.72→$8.45. SLV (mom=+36.04% composite, but 1m=-22.47%, dd63=-41.74%, vol20=55.70%, below MA50 $77.84) — acute correction deepening; MA200 buffer intact ($62.47 vs $51.72, +20.7%). GLD (mom=+11.85%, 1m=-16.05%, dd63=-18.52%, below MA50) — structural 12m=+45.08% thesis intact but near-term headwinds from dollar strength/tariff-driven risk-off.

GO/NO-GO: NON-REBALANCE DAY — Rebalance due = false. Days since last rebalance checkpoint = 6 (minimum cadence = 7 days; next eligible rebalance window = 2026-03-25, TOMORROW). SPY MA200 breach confirmed for 2nd consecutive session — pre-committed trigger for RISK-OFF regime upgrade at 2026-03-25 rebalance now activated. No emergency kill-switch breach: SLV close=$62.47 vs MA200=$51.72 (buffer=+20.7%, above emergency floor); GLD close=$404.04 vs MA200=$375.08 (buffer=+7.7%, narrowing but above floor). EPS acceleration trajectories confirmed via qualitative refresh: XOM Q1-FY2026=$1.65 growing to $2.00 by Q3; CVX $1.69→$2.12; GOOGL $2.76→$3.21; KLAC $9.14→$12.10; LMT $6.72→$8.45; TSM $3.26→$3.99. DO NOTHING — hold portfolio exactly unchanged. Mandatory full rebalance evaluation tomorrow 2026-03-25 with RISK-OFF regime classification and GLD/SLV sizing review as pre-committed agenda items.

MA200 buffer narrows to +20.7% ($62.47 vs $51.72). Pre-committed evaluation at 2026-03-25 rebalance: if SLV fails to show recovery signal, trim from 4% to 2% or exit.

1m=-16.05%, dd63=-18.52%, below MA50 ($404.04 vs $455.73, gap=-11.3%). MA200 buffer now only +7.7% ($404.04 vs $375.08) — tightest buffer in this run series. Pre-committed 2026-03-25 review: if GLD remains below MA50 and SPY MA200 breach persists (3+ sessions), reduce from 29% toward 22-25%.

1m=-8.28%, below MA50 ($338.45 vs $347.18, gap=-2.5%) — persistent 6-session MA50 breach. Tariff/Taiwan geopolitical overhang unresolved heading into April 2 reciprocal tariff deadline. dd63=-14.85%, vol20=36.68%. Monitoring flag active; above MA200 preserves hold thesis for now.

1m=-6.22%, below MA50 ($616.25 vs $626.33, gap=-1.6%) for second consecutive session. 3m=+30.66% structural momentum intact but 1m deterioration warrants monitoring. Defense rearmament thesis not impaired; no emergency threshold breached.

thesis: NON-REBALANCE DAY: Rebalance due = false. Days since last rebalance checkpoint = 6; minimum cadence = 7 days. Next eligible rebalance window = 2026-03-25 (TOMORROW). No emergency kill-switch threshold has been breached across any of the 8 holdings. Portfolio weighted momentum composite ~29% vs SPY mom=+2.31% — +27pp alpha spread is the primary signal that current positioning continues to outperform the mixed/defensive regime. SPY MA200 breach (close=$655.38 vs MA200=$657.20) now confirmed for 2 consecutive sessions — pre-committed trigger for RISK-OFF regime upgrade and mandatory GLD/SLV sizing review is formally activated for tomorrow's rebalance. The 2026-03-25 rebalance will be a high-conviction event.

FACTOR EDGE: KLAC (mom=+45.04%, above both MAs, 1m=+1.60%, FY2026 EPS $9.14→$12.10) remains the highest-quality momentum name. XOM (mom=+34.06%, above both MAs, 3m=+39.00%, dd63=-5.48%) and CVX (mom=+28.92%, above both MAs, 3m=+40.25%, dd63=-5.28%) form the most robust factor cluster — energy sector carries the fund's quality anchor. EPS estimate refreshes confirm all six equity trajectories accelerating: XOM $1.65→$2.00, CVX $1.69→$2.12, GOOGL $2.76→$3.21, KLAC $9.14→$12.10, LMT $6.72→$8.45, TSM $3.26→$3.99. Combined GLD (29%) + SLV (4%) = 33% precious metals at deepest 1m drawdown (-16%/-22%) since the fund launched, but MA200 buffers of +7.7%/+20.7% remain above kill-switch floors. Pre-committing to GLD reduction from 29% to 22-24% at tomorrow's rebalance if MA50 recovery is not evidenced.

VARIANT VIEW: Holding unchanged rather than emergency-exiting GLD or SLV today: both the SPY MA200 breach and the precious metals simultaneous drawdown are consistent with a tariff-driven USD-strengthening / forced liquidation episode rather than a structural commodity bear reversal. The GLD MA200 buffer has narrowed to +7.7% — this is the tightest level in this run series and warrants urgency at tomorrow's rebalance, but does not cross an emergency threshold today. The critical distinction is that TLT is also declining (not rallying), which is inconsistent with a genuine risk-off flight-to-safety; instead it signals stagflationary forced selling across asset classes — a regime where commodity/energy/defense positioning historically recovers once the selling exhausts. Bear case at 30% probability: sustained precious metals bear (requires genuine hawkish pivot or structural USD rally — not evidenced). The 2026-03-25 rebalance will operationalize the pre-committed GLD trim with a full IC scoring review.
risks: MACRO: SPY MA200 breach now confirmed 2 consecutive sessions — if sustained through tomorrow, full RISK-OFF regime classification triggers mandatory defensive repositioning. TLT below both MAs removes flight-to-safety bond bid; stagflation backdrop with April 2 reciprocal tariff deadline creates binary event risk for TSM and KLAC (semis/ADR exposure). GLD MA200 buffer has narrowed to +7.7% — a further -8% move in GLD would breach MA200 and trigger emergency exit review mid-cycle. Combined GLD+SLV=33% means a further -10% move costs ~330bps of NAV.

IDIOSYNCRATIC: SLV dd63=-41.74%, vol20=55.70% — a 1-sigma daily move (~3.5%) could consume another 5-7% of the remaining MA200 buffer within 2-3 sessions. GLD MA200 floor at $375.08 is now only 7.7% away (was 10.3% yesterday, 14.1% last week) — acceleration of the buffer erosion is a concern. TSM 1m=-8.28% below MA50 — any tariff announcement specifically targeting Taiwan-origin semiconductor imports before April 2 could trigger a sharp gap-down. GOOGL 3m=-1.59% drifting below zero; DOJ antitrust remedy hearing risk adds binary event overhang.

EXECUTION/LIQUIDITY: No execution actions today. All 8 holdings remain highly liquid. Pre-committed monitoring checkpoints for 2026-03-25 rebalance: (1) GLD close vs MA200 $375.08 — if breached before rebalance, emergency trim review activated; (2) GLD reduction from 29% to 22-24% pre-committed if GLD remains below MA50 AND SPY MA200 breach persists; (3) SLV trim from 4% to 2% or exit pre-committed if SLV shows no recovery signal; (4) TSM — if 1m deteriorates further below -10%, trim or replace evaluation at rebalance; (5) SPY MA200 breach confirmed for 3rd consecutive session tomorrow = mandatory RISK-OFF rebalance framework activation.
falsifiable_checks: GLD KILL-SWITCH: If GLD closes below MA200 $375.08 at any point before 2026-03-25 rebalance, emergency trim review is triggered regardless of schedule. Current buffer: $404.04 vs $375.08 = +7.7%. GLD must show recovery above MA50 $455.73 within 14 days (by 2026-04-07) or weight is reduced from 29% to 22-24% at the next rebalance. GLD 1w=-12.03% — if weekly rate of decline continues at this pace for 2 more sessions, MA200 breach probability exceeds 50% by end of week.

SPY REGIME TRIGGER: SPY close=$655.38 vs MA200=$657.20 — MA200 breach confirmed for 2 consecutive sessions. If SPY remains below MA200 through 2026-03-25 (3rd consecutive session), regime is formally upgraded to RISK-OFF and the 2026-03-25 rebalance becomes a mandatory defensive repositioning event: GLD trim 29%→22-24%, SLV trim 4%→2%, proceeds to XOM/CVX increase or LMT increase. SLV FLOOR: SLV close=$62.47 vs MA200=$51.72 — buffer=+20.7%. If SLV 1w return deteriorates below -15% (currently -12.82%), pre-rebalance emergency review is triggered on 2026-03-25. KLAC CONFIRMATION: KLAC above both MAs (close=$1,511.43 vs MA50=$1,474.89, MA200=$1,150.41) — must maintain above MA50 through April 2 tariff event to retain 10% weight; breach of MA50 triggers trim to 7-8%.
trade_of_the_day.why_now: Base case: Do nothing. This is a non-rebalance day (day 6 of 7-day minimum cadence). No holding has crossed an emergency kill-switch threshold. The portfolio's +27pp alpha spread over SPY is the primary signal that current positioning is working. Bull case (40%): The SPY MA200 breach and precious metals correction are transient tariff-driven panic events; GLD and SLV stabilize near MA200 support zones, KLAC and XOM continue to lead, and the energy/defense/quality-tech mix outperforms through the April 2 tariff deadline resolution. The 2026-03-25 rebalance executes a calibrated GLD trim (29%→22-24%) and SLV reduction (4%→2%) that improves risk efficiency without abandoning the commodity thesis. Bear case (35%): SPY MA200 breach is sustained, GLD accelerates toward MA200 ($375), SLV breaches MA200 ($51.72), and tariff escalation hits TSM and KLAC — 33% precious metals allocation plus tech/semis exposure creates compounding drawdown. Bear case is now elevated vs prior sessions due to the narrowing GLD MA200 buffer (+7.7%) and 2-session SPY MA200 breach. Expected edge of holding vs emergency trading today: saves 30-50bps round-trip costs, avoids selling GLD/SLV at potential panic lows, and preserves the formal IC rebalance framework for tomorrow 2026-03-25 where a full multi-factor scoring review with 1 more session of data will be conducted. The 2026-03-25 rebalance is now a mandatory high-conviction event with pre-committed action triggers already defined.

constraints_check.notes: FACTOR SCORES (0-100): KLAC=82 (mom 45%, above both MAs, EPS ramp $9.14→$12.10, quality anchor); XOM=79 (mom 34%, above both MAs, 3m=+39%, dd63=-5.5%, low vol); CVX=76 (mom 29%, above both MAs, 3m=+40%, lowest vol=19.4%, defensive ballast); TSM=68 (mom 32%, above MA200, EPS $3.26→$3.99, tariff discount applied -5pts); GLD=58 (mom +12%, above MA200 only, 12m=+45%, MA200 buffer=+7.7% — PENALTY applied for MA50 breach and narrowing buffer); LMT=65 (mom 25%, above MA200, 3m=+31%, defense rearmament thesis intact, 1m=-6.2% flag); GOOGL=60 (mom 25%, above MA200 only, 3m=-1.6% soft flag, EPS $2.76→$3.21, antitrust overhang -5pts); SLV=42 (mom composite +36% but 1m=-22.5%, dd63=-42%, vol=56%, MA50 breach -12pts — retained at reduced 4% for structural 12m=+108% momentum and MA200 buffer). RISK BUDGET: Energy cluster (XOM+CVX)=29% — max 80% sector ok; Technology (GOOGL+KLAC+TSM)=28% — ok; Commodities (GLD+SLV)=33% — ok; Defense (LMT)=10% — ok. Max position = GLD 29% <= 40% — ok. Crypto = 0% <= 10% — ok. Total = 100%. SCENARIO STRESS: Equity index shock (-10% broad): estimated portfolio impact ~-4.8% (equity names 53% weight avg beta ~0.9); precious metals provide partial hedge. Rates shock (TLT -5%): minimal direct exposure; energy names could benefit from stagflation. Commodity shock (GLD/SLV -15%): ~-5% portfolio impact — largest single-factor risk at 33% combined weight. USD shock (+5%): negative for TSM (ADR), GLD, SLV; partially offset by XOM/CVX (USD-denominated oil revenues). EXECUTION PLAN (2026-03-25 rebalance): (1) GLD trim 29%→22-24% if MA50 breach persists and SPY MA200 breach confirmed 3rd session — pre-committed; (2) SLV trim 4%→2% if no recovery signal — pre-committed; (3) freed weight (~6-8%) reallocated to XOM increase (+3-4%) and/or LMT increase (+2-3%) as defensive/energy anchors. KILL-SWITCH TRIGGERS: GLD close <$375.08 (MA200) = emergency trim; SLV 1w return <-15% = pre-rebalance review; SPY MA200 breach 3rd session = mandatory RISK-OFF repositioning; KLAC close <MA50 $1,474.89 = trim from 10% to 7-8%. MONITORING: Next checkpoint 2026-03-25 mandatory high-conviction rebalance.
